100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 9, 1926, the British Empire celebrated the impressive achievements of its fleet as the Royal Navy's new aircraft carrier, HMS Eagle, was officially commissioned. In the United States, the fallout from the Scopes Trial continued to dominate headlines, sparking national debates on evolution and education. Meanwhile, in Germany, the economic crisis deepened as the government struggled to stabilize hyperinflation, affecting the daily lives of citizens.
